annotations.label: Tangent space (informal)

anchor.snippet: a real vector space that intuitively contains the possible directions in which one can tangentially pass through

annotations.note: `TangentSpace I x` is defined (as a type synonym for the model space `E`) with its vector-space structure, giving the tangent space at a point of a manifold.

annotations.label: Tangent vectors

anchor.snippet: are called the tangent vectors at

annotations.note: Tangent vectors are exactly the terms of `TangentSpace I x`.

annotations.label: Dimension of tangent space equals manifold dimension

anchor.snippet: The dimension of the tangent space at every point of a connected manifold is the same as that of the manifold itself

annotations.note: Holds definitionally since `TangentSpace I x := E` equals the model space, but no separate `dim`-of-manifold lemma asserts the equality.

annotations.label: Tangent plane of a sphere

anchor.snippet: one can picture the tangent space at a point as the plane that touches the sphere at that point

annotations.note: `range_mfderiv_coe_sphere` identifies the tangent space of the unit sphere at `v` with the orthogonal complement `(ℝ ∙ v)ᗮ`.

annotations.label: Tangent space of an algebraic variety

anchor.snippet: there is an intrinsic definition of the tangent space at a point of an algebraic variety

annotations.note: The algebraic cotangent space `m ⧸ m²` of a local ring is formalized, but the (Zariski) tangent space of a variety/scheme point itself is not defined.

annotations.label: Non-singular and singular points

anchor.snippet: are called non-singular points; the others are called singular points

annotations.note: Non-singularity is captured algebraically by `IsRegularLocalRing` (regular local ring of the point), but a geometric singular/non-singular-point predicate on varieties is not defined.

annotations.label: Vector field

anchor.snippet: A vector field attaches to every point of the manifold a vector from the tangent space at that point

annotations.note: Vector fields are represented as sections `Π (x : M), TangentSpace I x` and have substantial API (e.g. `VectorField.mlieBracket`), but there is no standalone `VectorField` definition.

annotations.label: Tangent bundle

anchor.snippet: to form a new differentiable manifold with twice the dimension of the original manifold

annotations.note: `TangentBundle I M` is defined as the total space `Σ x, TangentSpace I x` and is given a manifold structure in `VectorBundle.Tangent`.

annotations.label: Tangent space via tangent curves

anchor.snippet: equivalence classes of such curves are known as tangent vectors of

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the tangent space as a type synonym for `E`, not as equivalence classes of tangent curves.

annotations.label: Vector-space structure via chart

anchor.snippet: turns out to be bijective and may be used to transfer the vector-space operations

annotations.note: The vector-space structure on `TangentSpace` is built in (it is `E`), and chart-change linear maps `tangentCoordChange` exist, but no proposition transfers operations across a chart bijection of curve-classes.

annotations.label: Smooth functions on a manifold

anchor.snippet: if and only if for every coordinate chart

annotations.note: `ContMDiff I I' n f` defines smoothness of maps between manifolds via charts, with the smooth-function bundled type `C^n⟮I, M; 𝕜⟯`.

annotations.label: Smooth functions form a real associative algebra

anchor.snippet: is a real associative algebra with respect to the pointwise product and sum of functions

annotations.note: Instances `ContMDiffMap.commRing` and `ContMDiffMap.algebra` make `C^n⟮I, N; 𝓘(𝕜,A), A⟯` a (commutative) algebra under pointwise operations.

annotations.label: Derivation at a point

anchor.snippet: that satisfies the Leibniz identity

annotations.note: `Derivation R A M` is a linear map satisfying the Leibniz rule (`leibniz'`), specialized to a point by `PointDerivation`.

annotations.label: Derivation vanishes on constant functions

anchor.snippet: For every identically constant function

annotations.note: `Derivation.map_algebraMap` (and `map_one_eq_zero`) shows a derivation sends constants (algebra-map elements) to zero.

annotations.label: Tangent space as derivations

anchor.snippet: the set of all derivations at

annotations.note: `PointDerivation I x := Derivation 𝕜 C^∞⟮I,M;𝕜⟯⟨x⟩ 𝕜` is the space of derivations at a point, offered as an alternative tangent-space definition.

annotations.label: Zariski tangent space

anchor.snippet: is the collection of all

annotations.note: The cotangent space `CotangentSpace R = m ⧸ m²` is formalized; the Zariski tangent space (its dual) is obtainable but not explicitly named.

annotations.label: Isomorphism of curve and derivation tangent spaces

anchor.snippet: is a vector space isomorphism between the space of the equivalence classes

annotations.note: Mathlib does not prove an isomorphism between the curve-based `TangentSpace` and `PointDerivation` (the docs note this identification fails in general).

annotations.label: Quotient isomorphic to cotangent space

anchor.snippet: can be shown to be isomorphic to the cotangent space

annotations.note: Algebraically `CotangentSpace R` is by definition `m ⧸ m²`, but the manifold germ-ring version `I_x ⧸ I_x²` and its identification with a cotangent space are not formalized.

annotations.label: Tangent space via cotangent space

anchor.snippet: may then be defined as the dual space of

annotations.note: The cotangent space `m ⧸ m²` is defined and a dual could be taken, but the tangent space as the dual of `I_x ⧸ I_x²` in the manifold setting is not formalized.

annotations.label: Equivalence of derivation and cotangent definitions

anchor.snippet: an equivalence between tangent spaces defined via derivations and tangent spaces defined via cotangent spaces

annotations.note: No equivalence between the derivation and cotangent definitions of the tangent space is proved in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tangent spaces of an open subset of R^n

anchor.snippet: the tangent spaces are all naturally identified with

annotations.note: `NormedSpace.fromTangentSpace` gives the canonical isomorphism `TangentSpace 𝓘(𝕜,E) v ≃L[𝕜] E`, identifying tangent spaces of a normed space (hence an open subset) with the space itself.

annotations.label: Directional derivative in R^n

anchor.snippet: one defines the corresponding directional derivative at a point

annotations.note: `lineDeriv 𝕜 f x v = deriv (fun t ↦ f (x + t • v)) 0` is exactly the directional derivative of `f` at `x` along `v`.

annotations.label: Derivations in R^n are directional derivatives

anchor.snippet: there is a one-to-one correspondence between vectors

annotations.note: Mathlib does not establish the bijection between vectors of `ℝⁿ` and derivations of smooth functions at a point.

annotations.label: Directional derivative on a manifold

anchor.snippet: define the directional derivative

annotations.note: The directional derivative is expressible via `mfderiv` or evaluation of a `PointDerivation`, but there is no dedicated manifold directional-derivative definition.

annotations.label: Coordinate basis of the tangent space

anchor.section: Basis of the tangent space at a point
anchor.snippet: then one can define an ordered basis

annotations.note: Mathlib has no coordinate basis `∂/∂xⁱ` of the tangent space for general manifolds.

annotations.label: Tangent vector as linear combination of basis

anchor.section: Basis of the tangent space at a point
anchor.snippet: as a linear combination of the basis tangent vectors

annotations.note: Without a coordinate basis of the tangent space, the expansion of a tangent vector in such a basis is not formalized.

annotations.label: Derivative (pushforward) of a smooth map

anchor.snippet: induces natural linear maps between their corresponding tangent spaces

annotations.note: `mfderiv` gives the induced linear map between tangent spaces and `tangentMap` the pushforward between tangent bundles of a smooth map.

annotations.label: Local diffeomorphism and inverse function theorem
annotations.note: The direction local-diffeomorphism ⇒ `mfderiv` is a linear iso is proved, but the converse (manifold inverse function theorem: invertible differential ⇒ local diffeomorphism) is still a TODO.
